Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a description of a process and we need to model it as a POWL Partially Ordered Workflow Language model

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify the activities and create Transition objects for them except silent ones which are SilentTransition Identify the control flow structures loops and exclusive choices Identify the partial orders concurrent and sequential constraints

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Start with verifying the Service Request SR and then Contract Setup CS must be done So we have SR to CS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After CS two tasks can be done in parallel Datacenter Allocation DA and Access Control Initialization ACI So we have a partial order with nodes DA ACI and no order between them but both after CS

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then after both DA and ACI are done we have a loop The loop is between Base Network Configuration BNC and Debug Reconfigure DR The loop structure is BNC DR This means execute BNC then either exit or execute DR and then BNC again

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After the loop we have an exclusive choice between Containerized Deployment CD and Monolithic Deployment MD This is an XOR exclusive choice between CD and MD

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After the deployment we have two tasks Quality Assurance QA and Security Audit SA that can be done concurrently They are in a partial order with no order between them but both must be done before Go Live Approval GLA

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Finally after QA and SA are both done we do Go Live Approval GLA

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: However note that the entire process is a sequence of steps with some parts having internal concurrency or loops

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can structure the entire process as a partial order of the main phases but note that the description gives a clear sequence SR to CS to DA and ACI concurrently to Loop BNC DR to XOR CD MD to QA and SA concurrently to GLA

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets create the nodes step by step

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Create all the transitions activities and silent transitions if needed Create the loop node with BNC and DR Create the XOR node with CD and MD Note the description says after the loop we choose one of CD or MD So the XOR has two children CD and MD

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now we have the following nodes for the main partial order SR CS DA ACI loop XOR QA SA GLA

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note that DA and ACI are concurrent and are both after CS and before the loop Actually the description says Once DA and ACI are both completed the process moves to a looped configuration phase So the loop waits for both DA and ACI

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also the loop is followed by the XOR and then the XOR is followed by QA and SA which are concurrent and both must be done before GLA

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the partial order constraints are SR to CS CS to DA CS to ACI DA to loop ACI to loop loop to XOR XOR to QA XOR to SA QA to GLA SA to GLA But note DA and ACI are not ordered with respect to each other

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can create a StrictPartialOrder for the entire process adding the nodes and then the edges

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note that the loop and XOR are OperatorPOWL nodes and the others are Transition nodes

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Well create SR Transition SR CS Transition CS DA Transition DA ACI Transition ACI BNC Transition BNC DR Transition DR CD Transition CD MD Transition MD QA Transition QA SA Transition SA GLA Transition GLA loop OperatorPOWL Operator LOOP BNC DR xor OperatorPOWL Operator XOR CD MD Then create a StrictPartialOrder with nodes SR CS DA ACI loop xor QA SA GLA Then add the edges as above

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But note the partial order must include all these nodes However the loop and xor are nodes that contain sub models

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We do not need a silent transition for the exclusive choice because the XOR has two explicit transitions CD and MD But note the description does not say that one of the branches might be skipped So we do not need a silent transition in the XOR
